data.title: Grupo Televisa reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Grupo Televisa press release (NYSE:TV): Q4 GAAP EPS of -Ps.3.62
  • Revenues decreased by 5.2% to Ps.15,568.5 million compared with Ps.16,422.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Revenues in our MSO operations fell by 2.3%, mainly driven by a slightly lower subscriber base.
  • Revenues in our Enterprise Operations decreased by 6.3%.
  • Finally, Sky’s revenues declined by 12.4%, driven by the year-on-year RGUs decrease.

data.title: Southern Company targets 6% EPS growth in 2025 with $63B capital investment plan
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Chris Womack, CEO, highlighted that 2024 was a strong year with adjusted EPS reaching $4.05, representing a growth of 11% from 2023. He emphasized the company's focus on delivering "clean, safe, reliable, and affordable energy," supported by significant operational achievements across all business units.
  • The company is prioritizing growth opportunities in large electric loads, particularly data centers, which represent 80% of the potential 50,000 MW incremental load by the mid-2030s. Over 10,000 MW of commitments are already secured, with advanced discussions for more.
  • Southern Power's renewable portfolio includes 13,000 MW of capacity, with 500 MW of solar under construction. Management is exploring upgrades to natural gas facilities and potential new brownfield projects to meet future demand.
  • CFO Dan Tucker reported adjusted EPS of $4.05 for 2024, achieving the top end of guidance. He attributed the performance to investments in state-regulated utilities and weather-related impacts. He also outlined a $63 billion capital investment plan for 2025-2029, marking a $14 billion increase from the previous forecast.
  • Tucker noted that the company is on track for long-term state-regulated rate base growth of 7% annually, up from prior projections of 6%.

Outlook

  • Southern Company issued an adjusted EPS guidance range of $4.20 to $4.30 for 2025, with a midpoint of $4.25, reflecting 6% growth from the 2024 guidance midpoint.
  • Long-term EPS growth guidance remains at 5%-7%, but management suggested that sustainable growth near the high end of this range is achievable, particularly as incremental capital investments materialize.
  • Retail electricity sales for 2025 are projected to grow 2%-3% compared to 2024, driven by strong commercial demand, especially from data centers.
  • Management signaled potential $10 billion to $15 billion in incremental regulated capital investments, contingent on regulatory approvals, with further clarity expected later this year.

Financial Results

  • Southern Company reported adjusted EPS of $4.05 for 2024. Commercial sales were notably strong, with data center power usage up 17% year-over-year.
  • The company added 57,000 residential electric customers and 26,000 natural gas customers during 2024, reflecting robust growth in the Southeast.
  • Base capital investments of $63 billion over the next five years will focus primarily on state-regulated utilities, constituting 95% of the plan. Key drivers include transmission system upgrades and new projects.
  • The financing plan includes average annual equity needs of approximately $800 million to maintain investment-grade credit ratings and target 17% FFO-to-debt by the latter part of the forecast horizon.

Q&A

  • Carly Davenport, Goldman Sachs, asked about earnings growth and the capital investment split between Georgia Power and natural gas pipelines. CFO Dan Tucker explained that most investments are tied to Georgia Power, with some allocations for FERC-regulated natural gas pipeline expansions.
  • Julien Dumoulin-Smith, Jefferies, inquired about the earnings impact of Southern Power's asset recontracting. Tucker stated that contracts expiring in the 2030s could be renewed at significantly higher rates due to market conditions.
  • Steve Fleishman, Wolfe Research, sought clarity on the potential EPS growth rebase in 2027. Tucker attributed this to back-end loaded revenue growth and anticipated stabilization of interest costs.
  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan, explored the potential for coal retirements and carbon capture technologies. Management emphasized ongoing research into gas carbon capture and noted that coal retirements would depend on future EPA regulations.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a cautiously optimistic tone, focusing on the company's ability to capitalize on long-term growth opportunities while managing regulatory and financing challenges.
  • Management conveyed confidence in achieving sustainable growth, particularly in regulated utilities and complementary businesses like Southern Power. Tucker's comments on "adding durability" to the earnings trajectory underscored this confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more assertive in outlining capital investment plans and projecting future growth potential.
  • Analysts raised concerns about regulatory approvals and supply chain constraints but acknowledged the company's disciplined approach to managing these risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EPS growth guidance for 2025 increased to 6%, compared to the flat guidance issued in Q3 2024.
  • Capital investment plans grew by $14 billion, with a stronger emphasis on transmission system upgrades and data center projects.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from storm recovery costs in Q3 to the long-term growth trajectory and incremental capital opportunities.
  • Management's tone showed increased confidence, particularly in achieving high-end growth targets and leveraging complementary businesses.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential regulatory delays or unfavorable rulings could impact the $10 billion to $15 billion incremental capital investments.
  • Supply chain constraints, particularly for natural gas turbines and transmission equipment, pose challenges to meeting growth targets.
  • Rising interest rates may continue to pressure financing costs as older debts are refinanced.
  • Analysts raised concerns about customer affordability, given the magnitude of upcoming capital expenditures and rate base growth.

Final Takeaway

Southern Company delivered a strong 2024 performance, with adjusted EPS reaching $4.05 and robust growth across its regulated utility and Southern Power segments. Management outlined a $63 billion capital investment plan through 2029, targeting 7% annual rate base growth and 6% EPS growth for 2025. With significant opportunities in data centers and renewable energy, the company is well-positioned for long-term growth, though regulatory approvals and supply chain risks remain key challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
